critique: The result actually proved is a polynomial-exponent bracket, not an exact minimax rate: the upper bound carries a finite-VC logarithmic factor and the lower bound has no matching logarithmic converse. The achievability theorem is not over the full overlap class; it is over collections with common foldwise nuisance-rate constants, and the note explicitly says overlap decay alone is insufficient for that upper bound. The converse is a two-point construction under local policy-richness and, for feasible compatibility, known-logging finite-cell witnesses, so it establishes the calibrated weak-arm exponent rather than a universal characterization for arbitrary finite-VC policy classes. The delivered beta calibration and matching polynomial lower/conditional upper are enough for a field-level rate result, but not for flagship-level sharp minimax resolution.
